forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+color: 9900cc
+description: 'Move ALL UI-defined commands into plugins / the CommandService. The UI remains PRESENTATION: it renders/updates the OS menu, palette, jump overlay, context menus by interpreting the service catalogue, and dispatches existing command ids — it must NEVER define commands. Webview-only behaviors use a uniform handler-bus pattern (the ai/commands.ts module bus is the precedent): the plugin owns the command definition (id/name/keys/menu/availability), the webview registers a HANDLER keyed by the plugin command id. Commands with a real backend op route execute to that op (nav spatial_navigate/drill/focus). Do NOT overlay builtin YAML (compose_registry!/nav.yaml) onto the service snapshot — that hack was tried and rejected. The service→OS-menu plumbing (RegisterCommand.menu → CommandMetadata.menu → command_metadata_to_def → CommandDef.menu → menu.rs collect_menu_entries) is correct and must not change.'
+name: UI commands → plugins cleanup
reverse_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,3 +0,0 @@
-color: 9900cc
-description: 'Move ALL UI-defined commands into plugins / the CommandService. The UI remains PRESENTATION: it renders/updates the OS menu, palette, jump overlay, context menus by interpreting the service catalogue, and dispatches existing command ids — it must NEVER define commands. Webview-only behaviors use a uniform handler-bus pattern (the ai/commands.ts module bus is the precedent): the plugin owns the command definition (id/name/keys/menu/availability), the webview registers a HANDLER keyed by the plugin command id. Commands with a real backend op route execute to that op (nav spatial_navigate/drill/focus). Do NOT overlay builtin YAML (compose_registry!/nav.yaml) onto the service snapshot — that hack was tried and rejected. The service→OS-menu plumbing (RegisterCommand.menu → CommandMetadata.menu → command_metadata_to_def → CommandDef.menu → menu.rs collect_menu_entries) is correct and must not change.'
-name: UI commands → plugins cleanup
